title: viola sororia var. novae-angliae
intro: Viola sororia var. novae-angliae, also known as the New England violet, is a plant species within the viola sororia genus. This perennial plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Viola sororia var. novae-angliae include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or using the plant in topical applications for skin care.
